steps.title: Calculate calcium from ice cream
steps.content:
There are 4 cups of ice cream, each containing 276 mg of calcium. Total calcium from ice cream:
$$4 \times 276 = 1,104 \text{ mg}$$

steps.title: Find calcium per banana
steps.content:
The batch contains 1,114 mg total. Calcium from the 2 bananas is:
$$1,114 - 1,104 = 10 \text{ mg}$$
Calcium in 1 banana:
$$\frac{10}{2} = 5 \text{ mg}$$
That matches Choice A — $5$.
